channel.title: Steel on My Personal Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Steel on My Personal Blog

item.title: Steel and Oak Furniture
item.description:
Let’s change gears once again: furniture.

A series of furniture pieces made from steel and laminated oak.

Inspired by a collection from a well-known northern French retailer’s catalog from years ago 🤣.

Quality Materials

Except I made these with real materials:

  • 2mm tube in 40x40 and 45x45 for benches and table
  • 40x20 tube for the coffee table
  • Laminated oak tops in 22mm and 44mm for table and benches
  • Removable extension integrated under the table
  • Edges trimmed with router (my new best friend!)

Built to Last

Very heavy construction that will last. The “client” seems delighted 😁

item.title: ATOS IT Rack Doors
item.description:
One of the last series of laser-cut metal doors that BULL/ATOS put on their Sequana racks.

As with the metal facades of the Bullion B (last post), all this has been abandoned and replaced by plastic… a pity but understandable…

These 40 doors will serve as a base for new creations, furniture, lamps and other wall decorations

item.title: Brackets for Roses and Wisteria
item.description:
Let’s change things up a bit… no salvage or recycling this time.

Steel brackets and supports for wisteria and roses to replace the old pressure-treated wood ones.

Powder coating by Yvelines Sablage, always impeccable.

Some threaded rods were too short following the facade restoration. Had to innovate by extending them or having special nuts machined.

After spending the winter on the ground or sprawled on the roof, the beautiful vegetation was able to return to its new supports.

item.title: Coffee Table - Hitachi FMD
item.description:
In anticipation of a big sister

Prototype for a possible future 12-person meeting table. This small coffee table showcases what 8 Hitachi FMD (Flash Memory Module) disks look like. How the layout and steel structure should be assembled. 4mm tempered glass.

This one is available for sale.

For nostalgic electronics enthusiasts with graying hair… each purple capacitor is 30 farads, which is absolutely enormous (200 farads total) and even potentially dangerous when the disk is removed from its chassis and the capacitors are not discharged.

item.title: Teen Desks - Pinterest Style
item.description:
From a single Pinterest image, respecting classic or specific constraints to make these desks comfortable, the challenge is to create legs that are both original and stable. First welds with really acute angles, no possibility of grinding inside, the cuts must be perfect

Without any special treatment, just with Rutsol type oil. And here are 2 delighted teens

item.title: Coffee Table - 3PAR SSD
item.description:
Special dedication to AntemetA and the Stéphane BLANC years, great visionary of a beautiful era. 3PAR will remain one of the finest technologies and innovations of our generation.

This table in the lobby of ORNESS welcomes all enthusiasts.

I can still make the same one, I have exactly 12 SSDs left.

  • Dimensions: ~35 x 35 x 33 cm
  • Weight: 7kg

item.title: Infinite Cube
item.description:
Small scraps, of unequal lengths, from a previous project… No waste. On the occasion of acquiring a new metal band saw, a small precision exercise to make this infinite cube. Many sites give precise dimensions and save precious minutes.

Beware of heat-related deformations… The last weld to close the cube must be done under the constraint of clamps, otherwise not possible.

As decoration in a happy teenager’s room

item.title: TV/Amplifier Cabinet - DELL HPC
item.description:
During the relocation of this HPC from one room to another… the first failed blades were set aside…. once the components were recovered, the blade chassis should have gone to the industrial waste bin… They were ultimately used to create a TV/Amplifier cabinet. This is the first in a series to use this concept of metal chassis with IT components and glass on top.

Possibility to make more.

Dimensions: WDH: 142 x 53 x 41 cm
